etymology.description: 𠂈 (knife/dagger, phonetic) + 貝 (cowry, semantic) -> phonetic 丁 (nail) added -> phonetic 𠂈 corrupted to 十 -> final form
etymology.interpretation: The character 真 (zhēn, "true; real") originally involved 𠂈 as both phonetic and visual representation of a knife or dagger, with 貝 (bèi, "cowry") providing semantic context, presumably indicating value or authenticity. Over time, 丁 (dīng, "nail") was added as a secondary phonetic element, reflecting the joining or fixing aspect of 'true'. As forms evolved into script styles, 𠂈's shape was corrupted into 十, simplifying the visual complexity, while retaining the meaning of 'true' and 'real'.

examples.chinese: 这是真正的友情(這是真正的友情)。
他说的话句句都是真的(他說的話句句都是真的)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shuō de huà jùjù dōu shì zhēn de.

examples.chinese: 世界上的每一个事物都是真实的(世界上的每一個事物都是真實的)。
examples.pinyin: Shìjiè shàng de měi yī gè shìwù dōu shì zhēnshí de.
examples.english: Everything in the world is real.
